text:
Imane Khelif declared, ‘I am a woman’, after securing her first-everOlympicboxing medal on Saturday.

The Algerian produced an excellent performance in her 66kg quarter final against Hungary’sAnna Luca Hamorion Saturday, winning all three rounds convincingly.

Khelif will now face Thailand’s Janjaem Suwannapheng in the semi final on August 6 at 21.34pm, but is guaranteed at least a bronze medal regardless of the result of that fight.

Khelif’s participation inParishas beenmet with controversyafter she was disqualified from the Women’s World Championships last year for failing International Boxing Association (IBA)gendereligibility rules.

The 25-year-old has always competed in the women’s boxing division and has identified as female since birth.

The IBA has not specified why Khelif and Taiwan’s Lin Yu-ting,who is also competing in Paris, failed their gender eligibility tests but has continued to criticise the International Olympic Committee’s (IOC) decision to allow both fighters to compete in the women’s division in Paris.

Both Khelif and Lin competed at the Olympics in Tokyo three years ago but did not win a medal.

Khelif was overcome with tears of joy as she left the ring following her win over Hamori on Saturday.

In her only words to reporters in Paris after beating Hamori, Khelif said: ‘I am a woman.’

Speaking after her defeat, Hamori said: ‘I’m so proud of myself because I had to fight.

‘This was a hard fight, but I think I got to do everything I wanted before the fight. I think it was a good fight, I’m so proud of myself and I’m so grateful to be here.

‘This was a very good competition for me, that was my childhood dream. I’m so happy.

‘I wish good luck to my opponents and the others in the finals and I’m so thankful for my coaches, the Hungarian team, for my family and for everybody who gave me some support so thank you everyone.’

Khelif’s first appearance at this summer’s Olympics on Thursday also caused controversy after her opponent, Italy’sAngela Carini, abandoned their fight after just 46 seconds.

Carini took a heavy right hand from Khelif in the opening exchanges, went back to her corner and told her coach, ‘it’s not fair’ and ‘it hurts like hell’.

After Khelif’s hand was raised in victory, Carini refused to shake the Algerian’s hand and the Italian then cried in the middle of the ring.

Carini has since apologised for her decision not to shake Khelif’s hand after their contest.

‘All this controversy makes me sad,’ Carini told Italian newspaperGazzetta dello Sport.

‘I’m sorry for my opponent, too. If the IOC said she can fight, I respect that decision.’

Meanwhile, Skye Nicolson, the WBC women’s world featherweight champion,has also hit out at the ‘horrible’ treatment towards Khelif and Lin.

Nicolson, who called Carini’s reaction to her defeat to Khelif a ‘publicity stunt’,

‘I’ve actually fought and sparred both of the girls – they were born female,’ Nicolson said in a video on Instagram.

‘They were born with an XY chromosome, which is the male chromosome, but they were born with female bodies – they have the physical attributes of a female.

‘They have grown up as girls, as women. They have competed as women the whole time. These are not naturally born men who have decided to call themselves women or identify as women to fight women in the Olympics.

‘And while it is a bit of a grey area, I think the abuse and the power of the media, and people just jumping on the bandwagon without knowing all the details, is honestly horrible.

‘These girls have represented their country on numerous occasions for many, many years now, as female fighters, and they do not deserve this mistreatment. I think they have been so unfortunate enough to even be in the situation that they’re in and to get all this scrutiny as well.

‘These girls have been beat by girls, numerous times, both of them. If they were men and it was dangerous for girls to be fighting them they would be winning gold at everything – they’re not. I think that says it all.’

title: Imane Khelif says 'I am a woman' after winning Olympic boxing medal | Metro News
description: Imane Khelif declared, 'I am a woman', after securing her first-ever Olympic boxing medal on Saturday.
